title: Conjecture of Aldous, McDiarmid and Scott [4]
statement_text: For the $d$-dimensional hypercube $Q_d$ with independent random edge-capacities each distributed like $C$, the limiting optimal uniform multicommodity flow volume depends only on $E[C]$ (and not on the full distribution of $C$).
context_text: Aldous, McDiarmid and Scott [4] studied uniform multicommodity flows in the complete graph with random edge-capacities and posed a conjecture about the hypercube case. The present paper notes that capacity required for 'local escape' near a source is much less than that required for efficient long-path flows, making the limiting flow value depend only on $E[C]$, which the authors describe as establishing a strong form of this conjecture.
paper_contribution: Proves a strong form of this conjecture by establishing $\Phi_{\mathrm{opp}} \to E[C]$ and $2^{d-1}\Phi_{\mathrm{all}} \to E[C]$ both in probability and in expectation as $d \to \infty$, under the conditions $E[C] < \infty$ and $P[C > 0] > 1/2$ (Theorems 1 and 2).
notes: The exact statement of the conjecture from [4] is not reproduced verbatim in the paper; the statement above is reconstructed from the surrounding context. PDF source — math may be garbled.
paper_title: Uniform multicommodity flow in the hypercube with random edge capacities
